skill.name: vaex
skill.description: Use this skill to work with Vaex for large tabular datasets that do not fit in memory. It covers lazy DataFrames, out-of-core file formats, fast aggregation, visualization, and machine learning workflows.
skill.summary: Analyze large out-of-core DataFrames with Vaex guidance.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and blocker hits, but review found these are mostly Markdown fences, Vaex API examples, and placeholder filenames rather than executable skill code. The skill is documentation-only, but it includes cloud credential examples and a server command bound to 0.0.0.0, so publication should include a security warning.

medium_findings.title: Inline Cloud Credential Examples
medium_findings.description: The S3 and GCS examples show credentials passed directly in code or by credential JSON path. The values are placeholders, but copied patterns can lead users to expose secrets in prompts, notebooks, or repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly document secret-bearing parameters and a credential file path. They are placeholders, so the issue is risky guidance rather than confirmed credential disclosure.

medium_findings.title: Remote Vaex Server Exposed on All Interfaces
medium_findings.description: The server example binds to 0.0.0.0 and then documents websocket access. Without authentication, TLS, and network restrictions, this can expose dataset access or computation to unintended clients.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The bind address and remote connection example are explicit. The documentation does not state an auth model, so risk depends on deployment context.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command alerts point to Markdown backticks and Python examples. They are static documentation snippets, not skill code that executes shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched content is documentation in Markdown code blocks. No executable wrapper, script, hook, or command dispatcher was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: State File Loading Requires Trusted Inputs
low_findings.description: The references document saving and loading Vaex state JSON files. No malicious payload is present, but users should load state files only from trusted sources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines show state serialization workflows, but no evidence shows unsafe deserialization or code execution. The residual risk is trust-boundary guidance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Handling in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: Cloud storage examples include inline access key and secret key parameters and a credential JSON path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples directly contain secret-like parameter names and credential file usage, although only placeholders are shown.

dangerous_patterns.title: Public Network Bind Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The Vaex server example uses --host 0.0.0.0, which can expose the service beyond localhost.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command explicitly binds to all interfaces. Actual impact depends on firewall and authentication settings outside the skill.

content.value_statement: Large tabular datasets can exceed memory and slow normal DataFrame workflows. This skill provides Vaex patterns for lazy analysis, efficient file access, visualization, and ML preparation.
actual_capabilities: Guides loading CSV, HDF5, Arrow, Parquet, FITS, and cloud-hosted tabular data with Vaex.
Explains lazy expressions, virtual columns, filtering, selections, and groupby aggregations.
Provides performance guidance for caching, batching delayed operations, memory mapping, and format conversion.
Covers large dataset plots, histograms, heatmaps, selections, and visualization limits.
Documents Vaex machine learning transformers, encoders, PCA, clustering, and model pipeline state handling.
Includes I/O patterns for exports, remote storage, server access, and production data workflows.
limitations: The skill is reference guidance only and does not execute Vaex operations by itself.
Examples assume users already have Vaex and optional storage libraries installed.
Security settings for remote servers and cloud credentials require user review.
It does not validate datasets, credentials, file permissions, or storage policies.

use_cases.title: Prepare Out-of-Core Analysis
use_cases.description: Convert large CSV files to efficient formats and run descriptive statistics without loading all rows into memory.

use_cases.title: Explore Scientific Data
use_cases.description: Open large HDF5, Arrow, FITS, or Parquet datasets and create fast summaries, filters, and visualizations.

use_cases.title: Build Scalable ML Features
use_cases.description: Create virtual feature columns, encode categories, scale features, and manage Vaex pipeline state for large datasets.

prompt_templates.title: Open a Large Dataset
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the safest Vaex workflow to open my large file, inspect columns, and compute basic summary statistics.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Slow Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Vaex analysis plan and suggest lazy evaluation, file format, caching, and delayed execution improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Design Visual Exploration
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a Vaex visualization workflow for millions of rows, including limits, selections, histograms, and heatmaps.

prompt_templates.title: Create an ML Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Vaex machine learning pipeline with virtual features, encoders, scaling, validation, and state management.

output_examples.input: I have a 200 GB CSV and need summary statistics.
output: Recommended converting the CSV to HDF5 or Arrow before repeated analysis.
Outlined lazy loading, column inspection, missing value checks, and delayed aggregate execution.
Added cautions about disk space, chunk size, and preserving the original source file.

output_examples.input: I need to plot dense x and y columns from a billion-row table.
output: Suggested Vaex heatmaps and binned plots instead of raw scatter rendering.
Defined percentile limits, selections, and aggregation choices for interactive exploration.
Explained how to compare filtered subsets without materializing new DataFrames.

output_examples.input: I want production feature engineering with Vaex ML.
output: Mapped feature scaling, category encoding, and virtual columns into a repeatable pipeline.
Recommended saving state only from trusted workflows and reviewing state files before reuse.
Included validation checks for unseen categories, missing values, and train-test consistency.

best_practices: Prefer HDF5 or Arrow for repeated Vaex analysis on large local datasets.
Use virtual columns and delayed execution before materializing new columns.
Treat remote URLs, credentials, and state files as trusted inputs only.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real cloud access keys into prompts or shared notebooks.
Do not expose a Vaex server on all interfaces without access controls.
Do not use CSV as the main storage format for repeated large analyses.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps users apply Vaex patterns for large DataFrame analysis, visualization, I/O, and machine learning.

faq.question: Does the skill run Vaex code?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. The user or assistant must run code in a configured environment.

faq.question: Which file formats does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers CSV, HDF5, Apache Arrow, Parquet, FITS, and cloud storage paths.

faq.question: Can it help with datasets larger than RAM?
faq.answer: Yes. The references focus on lazy evaluation, memory mapping, and out-of-core workflows.

faq.question: Does it include machine learning guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Vaex ML transformers, encoders, scaling, clustering, and integration patterns.

faq.question: What security precautions are important?
faq.answer: Keep credentials out of prompts, restrict remote server access, and load state files only from trusted sources.
